feat: instant cached login with background authentication (#108)

* feat: instant cached login with background authentication

On repeat logins, hash the entered password with SHA-256 and compare
against a stored hash. If it matches, show the Dashboard with IndexedDB
cached data immediately while SSE authentication continues in the
background. Progress (e.g. "Authenticating browser…") is shown in the
top-bar SyncStatus area. When auth completes, the session is saved and
a full data refresh fires automatically.

On auth failure the top bar shows an error with a "Sign in again" link
(falls back to full login flow). The credential hash (never the plain
password) is saved after every successful login to enable the fast-path
on all subsequent visits.

- session.ts: add hashPassword(), saveCredentials(), loadCredentials()
- types.ts: add 'background-auth' AuthState variant
- App.tsx: wire background-auth handlers; render Dashboard for both
  'authenticated' and 'background-auth' states with token: string | null
- AuthFlow.tsx: fast-path on hash match; save credentials on success
- Dashboard.tsx: skip network fetch when token is null; trigger
  refreshAll() when token transitions null→string
- SyncStatus.tsx: show spinner + progress message / error during
  background auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update dashboard/src/components/AuthFlow.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback on instant login

- session.ts: upgrade hashPassword from SHA-256 to PBKDF2-SHA-256
  (100k iterations, fixed app salt) to slow offline brute-force;
  add loadCredentials shape validation and 30-day expiry; saveCredentials
  now persists createdAt timestamp
- App.tsx: fix race condition in handleBackgroundAuthComplete — use
  functional setAuthState to guard against stale completions after
  logout; call clearCredentials() on logout; drop errorMessage field
  from backgroundAuth prop (unused)
- AuthFlow.tsx: wrap hashPassword in try/catch so WebCrypto failure
  falls back to foreground auth instead of crashing
- Dashboard.tsx: guard intersection observer and handleMemberSelect
  against triggering lazy loaders when token is null (background-auth)
- SyncStatus.tsx: remove unused errorMessage from backgroundAuth prop
- AuthFlow.test.tsx: add 5 tests covering background-auth fast-path
  (hash match → onStartBackgroundAuth, progress routing, error routing,
  hash mismatch falls back to foreground, credentials saved on success)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: don't clear credentials on logout

Clearing the credential hash on logout defeats the fast-path feature —
the hash must survive logouts so the next login can skip the wait.
The 30-day expiry in loadCredentials() is sufficient for cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: allow empty contactId in loadCredentials validation

The backend SSE complete event does not include contactId, so it is
saved as '' and fetched dynamically via client.initialize(). The
validation added during code review rejected '' as malformed and
silently deleted the stored record on first read, preventing the
fast-path from ever triggering.

Also removes temporary debug logging added during diagnosis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: stale-while-revalidate for data refresh

Previously all panels cleared to skeleton placeholders during a refresh,
causing ~8s of blank content while data reloaded.

- refreshAll: preserve existing data when resetting lazy sections —
  sections with data transition to 'loading' (content still rendered)
  rather than 'idle' (skeleton). Sections scroll into view as before.
- SummaryTiles / ComplianceTable: only show skeleton on initial load
  (no data yet), not on subsequent refreshes where stale data exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: reorder dashboard sections and collapse low-priority panels

New order: Joining Journey → Disclosure Compliance → Suspensions →
Learning Records → Team Directory (collapsed) → Permits (collapsed) →
Awards & Recognitions (collapsed)

Team Directory, Permits, and Awards are collapsed by default with a
chevron toggle. Collapsing prevents lazy loading from triggering for
those sections until explicitly opened. Expanding fires the load
immediately if the section hasn't been fetched yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Expiring Soon / Not Started quick-filter to Learning Records table

Filter is enabled by default to highlight records needing attention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on instant-cached-login

- Guard lazy section load/retry callbacks (teamReviews, permits, awards)
  against null token during background-auth to prevent unauthorized requests
- Add keyboard accessibility to LazySection h2 toggle (role, tabIndex, onKeyDown)
- Validate `createdAt` as a finite number in loadCredentials() to prevent
  NaN comparison bypassing CREDENTIALS_MAX_AGE_MS expiry
- Restore global.fetch in afterEach in AuthFlow background-auth tests
  to prevent leaking mock into subsequent test files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct fetchMock.mock.calls type cast in traces-proxy test

vi.fn() without type params infers mock.calls as [], causing tsc to
reject destructuring. Cast through unknown to Parameters<typeof fetch>.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(cmi): fix MAME CMI boot hang — re-enable keyboard CPUs

The keyboard CPUs (mkbd:kbdcpu, alphakeys:kbdcpu) were suspended during
machine_reset() as a speed optimization, but the CMI firmware communicates
with them via ACIA serial links during boot and hangs at 'LOADING SYSTEM'
waiting for their response.

Also rebuilt with 120s boot turbo threshold (was 45s) to cover the full
~90-120s floppy boot from SYSV19.IMD.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: prevent permission changes for system tools that always auto-execute (#168)

System tools that never require approval (`update_document`,
`create_document`, `search_documents`, `create_briefing`,
`send_notification`) were accepting permission overrides via
assignee/task endpoints. These tools should be immutable — the API now
returns 400. A `disablePermissionChange` flag is added to tool metadata
so the iOS app can disable the permission change UI.

### Backend

- **`lib/ai/tools/index.ts`** — New `NO_PERMISSION_CHANGE_TOOLS`
readonly set
- **`POST /api/assignees`**, **`PUT /api/assignees/:id`**, **`PUT
/api/tasks/:id`** — Validate `toolPermissions` array against the set,
return 400 with tool names listed
- **`GET /api/tools`**, **`GET /api/assignees/:id/schema`** — Include
`disablePermissionChange: true` on applicable tools (omitted otherwise)

```ts
if (parsed.data.toolPermissions) {
  const forbidden = parsed.data.toolPermissions
    .filter((tp) => NO_PERMISSION_CHANGE_TOOLS.has(tp.toolName))
    .map((tp) => tp.toolName);
  if (forbidden.length > 0) {
    return errorJson(`Cannot change permissions for system tools: ${forbidden.join(", ")}`, 400);
  }
}
```

### iOS

- **`AgentTool`** — Added `disablePermissionChange: Bool?`
- **`ToolPermissionRow`** — Row remains fully visible and tappable; the
detail sheet opens so users can view tool info and current permission,
but permission option buttons are disabled (`.disabled(isDisabled)`)
preventing changes
- **`ToolDetailSheet`** — Accepts new `isDisabled` parameter; when true,
permission option buttons are greyed out and non-interactive
- **`AssigneeFormSheet`**, **`AssigneeDetailViewModel`**,
**`OnboardingViewModel`**, **`TaskToolPermissionsView`** — Filter out
`disablePermissionChange` tools before sending permissions to the API

### E2E Tests

- 400 rejection for all 5 system tools on assignee create/update and
task update
- `disablePermissionChange` property presence verified on `GET
/api/tools`
- Regular tool permission updates still succeed (positive case)

permission in assignee and task tool permission endpoint. Should return

disable_permission_change and iOS app will disable permission change if
had this property.


</details>

- Add Shizuku SDK dependency (api + provider, v13.1.5)
- Create ShizukuServiceToggler: wraps Shizuku lifecycle, permission,
  and shell commands (svc wifi/bluetooth, cmd location, svc data,

* fix: replace private Shizuku.newProcess with UserService AIDL approach

- Switch from Shizuku.newProcess (private in v13) to UserService pattern
  with ICommandService AIDL + CommandService implementation
- Add CommandService: Shizuku UserService that runs shell commands with
  elevated privileges, properly drains streams and destroys processes
- Inject CoroutineDispatcher via @IoDispatcher qualifier instead of
  hardcoding Dispatchers.IO (per project conventions)
- Remove INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS_FULL permission from ShizukuProvider
  (signature/system-only, blocks normal Shizuku binding)
- Enable AIDL build feature in build.gradle.kts
- Bind UserService when Shizuku becomes READY; unbind on binder death

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address 5 PR review issues (timeout, fallback, guard, focus, permission)

- CommandService: add 10s timeout with destroyForcibly() fallback
  to prevent hung shell commands from blocking the service thread
- MainViewModel.toggleService: handle setEnabled failure by falling
  back to openSettings (added test for this path)
- ShizukuServiceToggler: add isBound guard to prevent redundant
  bindUserService calls from multiple binder-received events
- ServiceCard timer: commit value on focus loss (onFocusChanged) in
  addition to IME Done, so navigating away preserves the edit
- MainScreen: request BLUETOOTH_CONNECT runtime permission at startup
  so BT state detection works instead of always hitting SecurityException

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address 5 PR review issues (context, auto-off fallback, cancellation, mobile data, readText)

- ShizukuServiceToggler: inject @ApplicationContext and use
  context.packageName instead of hardcoded PACKAGE_NAME constant
- ShizukuServiceToggler.setEnabled: rethrow CancellationException
  before the generic catch to preserve cooperative cancellation
- MainViewModel.evaluateAutoOff: check setEnabled return value and
  fall back to openSettings on failure (with AutoOffSettingsOpened event)
- SystemServiceController: use Settings.Global 'mobile_data' for
  isEnabled (accurate when on Wi-Fi), keep ConnectivityManager for
  isInUse; add ContentObserver to observe setting changes
- CommandService: remove blocking readText() before waitFor so the
  timeout is effective; our svc commands produce minimal output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pipe buffer, timer validation, auto-off re-fire prevention

- CommandService: use ProcessBuilder.Redirect.DISCARD to prevent pipe
  buffer blockage (available since API 33, our minSdk is 34)
- ServiceCard timer: filter input to digits-only, revert to last
  known-good value on focus loss if text is empty/non-numeric
- MainViewModel.evaluateAutoOff: change job check from isActive to
  null-check so completed jobs prevent re-triggering; auto-off only
  re-arms when service comes back in use then idle again
- Added test: auto-off does not re-fire until service returns to use

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use /dev/null redirect instead of Redirect.DISCARD (not on Android)

Redirect.DISCARD is Java 9+ JDK only and not available in Android's
ProcessBuilder. Use Redirect.to(File('/dev/null')) instead, which is
available on all Android versions and achieves the same effect.
